question_text: Explain why clothes dry faster on a warm, windy day than on a cold, still day, using the concept of evaporation.
correct_answer: Warm temperatures give water molecules more energy to escape as a gas (evaporate). Wind carries away the water vapour, preventing it from condensing back onto the clothes. Together, these increase the rate of evaporation.
explanation: Evaporation speeds up with heat and air movement. Warmth gives molecules more kinetic energy to escape the liquid, and wind removes the vapour so it cannot condense back.
